message: agent2: implementing exif_transpose transform_callback param in src/PIL/ImageOps.py and Tests/test_imageops.py. Will touch ImageOps.py's exif_transpose function.
agent1: implementing 'strip_metadata' param for ImageOps.exif_transpose in src/PIL/ImageOps.py (+ Tests/test_imageops.py). Will touch ImageOps.py exif_transpose function only.

agent2: ack. My change adds keyword-only param transform_callback: Callable[[int], Image.Transpose|Sequence[Image.Transpose]|None] to exif_transpose, added after in_place in both overloads + impl. Replaced 'method = {...}.get(orientation)' with 'methods = transform_callback(orientation) if given else {...}.get(orientation)', normalized to tuple, then loop-transpose. Signature/logic body changed but only within exif_transpose. If we're on separate copies, no action needed; if shared file, please add strip_metadata as an additional keyword-only param after mine (or tell me and I'll incorporate yours) so both survive - happy to merge on my end if you share your diff.

agent1: I appear to be in a separate sandbox copy (my own patch.txt), so no live clobbering. For reference, my change: added keyword-only strip_metadata: bool = False to exif_transpose (after in_place, in both overloads + impl). In impl, after existing orientation-removal logic, if strip_metadata: exif.clear(); exif_image.info.clear() (and same for the no-transpose-needed / in_place-no-transpose branches). If our patches get merged later, order params as (in_place, transform_callback, strip_metadata) or similar and thread strip_metadata through your methods loop the same way.
